description: Multi-tenant control plane that provisions isolated OpenClaw agent runtimes per Slack user. Each tenant gets a dedicated Docker container with separate filesystem, SSH keys, CLI auth state, and workspace. Three Node.js 22 TypeScript services: slack-relay (event ingestion + routing), control-plane (tenant lifecycle + API), and scheduler (idle stop + disk quota + queue reaping). Shared packages: shared-types, shared-config, docker-client, test-utils. SQLite+Prisma for persistence, Fastify for HTTP, execa for Docker CLI calls.

userStories.title: Monorepo scaffold with pnpm workspaces and TypeScript
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working monorepo scaffold so that all services and packages share consistent tooling and can be built together.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json has pnpm workspaces pointing to apps/* and packages/*
Root tsconfig.json with strict mode, Node.js 22 target (ES2023), path aliases for all packages
Each app (slack-relay, control-plane, scheduler) has its own package.json with 'build' and 'dev' scripts
Each package (shared-types, shared-config, docker-client, test-utils) has its own package.json
pnpm install succeeds from root without errors
pnpm -r build succeeds and compiles all packages and apps to dist/
Vitest is configured at root level with a vitest.config.ts
.gitignore excludes node_modules, dist, *.js.map
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared types package: tenant states, message types, and protocol types
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared TypeScript types so that all services use a single source of truth for tenant states, message payloads, and API contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared-types/src/tenant.ts exports TenantStatus enum with values: NEW, PROVISIONING, STARTING, ACTIVE, STOPPED, UNHEALTHY, FAILED, DELETING
packages/shared-types/src/tenant.ts exports Tenant interface matching all columns in the tenants DB table
packages/shared-types/src/message.ts exports MessageStatus enum: PENDING, PROCESSING, DELIVERED, FAILED
packages/shared-types/src/message.ts exports MessageQueueRow interface matching all columns in message_queue table
packages/shared-types/src/protocol.ts exports RelayMessageRequest and RelayMessageResponse interfaces matching §15 request/response schemas
packages/shared-types/src/health.ts exports TenantHealthResponse interface matching §19 health endpoint JSON shape
packages/shared-types/src/audit.ts exports AuditEventType enum with all 18 event types from §25
packages/shared-types/src/index.ts re-exports everything from all sub-modules
All other packages can import from @claw/shared-types without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Prisma schema with all 6 database tables and SQLite WAL mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a complete Prisma schema so that all database tables are defined, migrations can be applied, and WAL mode is enabled for concurrent reads.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma defines datasource with provider=sqlite and url from DATABASE_URL env
Tenant model has all 23 columns listed in §16 with correct types and defaults
MessageQueue model has all 10 columns with UNIQUE constraint on slack_event_id
StartupLock model has tenant_id as PK with locked_by, acquired_at, expires_at columns
AuditLog model has all 5 columns; tenant_id is nullable
Allowlist model has all 6 columns with index on (slack_team_id, slack_user_id, revoked_at)
ContainerImage model has all 7 columns with UNIQUE on tag
Index on tenants(slack_team_id, slack_user_id) is defined
Index on message_queue(tenant_id, status, created_at) is defined
pnpm prisma generate succeeds and emits a Prisma Client
A migration script (or seed) enables WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL
pnpm prisma migrate dev creates migrations without errors on a fresh SQLite file
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Docker client package: typed wrapper for Docker CLI via execa
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed Docker client package so that all Docker operations (run, start, stop, rm, inspect) are called through a consistent interface with proper error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/docker-client/src/index.ts exports a DockerClient class or object
dockerRun(options) calls 'docker run' via execa with: --name, --cpus, --memory, --memory-swap, --pids-limit, --ulimit nofile, --network, -v mounts, -e env vars, and -d flag
dockerStart(containerName) calls 'docker start' via execa
dockerStop(containerName, timeoutSeconds?) calls 'docker stop --time' via execa
dockerRm(containerName) calls 'docker rm -f' via execa
dockerInspect(containerName) calls 'docker inspect' and returns parsed JSON or null if not found
dockerExec(containerName, command) calls 'docker exec' via execa
All functions throw a typed DockerError with exitCode, stderr, and containerName fields on failure
auth-profiles.json bind-mount is supported: dockerRun accepts a readOnlyBindMounts option that adds ':ro' mounts
Unit tests mock execa and verify correct CLI argument construction for each function
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Shared config package with Zod validation for all environment variables
userStories.description: As a developer, I want validated configuration loaded at startup so that missing or malformed environment variables fail fast with clear error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/shared-config/src/control-plane.ts exports a Zod schema and parsed config for: CONTROL_PLANE_PORT (default 3200), DATABASE_URL, DATA_DIR, TENANT_IMAGE, LOG_LEVEL (default info), MAX_ACTIVE_TENANTS (default 10), ACTIVE_TENANTS_OVERFLOW_POLICY (enum: queue|reject, default queue)
packages/shared-config/src/slack-relay.ts exports a Zod schema for: SLACK_RELAY_PORT (default 3000), SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, CONTROL_PLANE_URL
packages/shared-config/src/scheduler.ts exports a Zod schema for: SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS (default 60000), IDLE_STOP_HOURS (default 48)
Each config module calls z.parse() at import time and throws a descriptive ZodError listing missing fields if validation fails
A .env.example file is created at repo root with all required variables documented, including a comment explaining auth-profiles.json bind-mount strategy (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
Unit tests verify that missing SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET causes a thrown error at config load time
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: Fastify app scaffold with startup reconciliation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working Fastify app for the control plane so that it starts cleanly, registers all routes, and reconciles any crashed state from a prior run.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/control-plane/src/index.ts creates a Fastify app with Pino logger using JSON format
App opens Prisma client on startup and runs 'prisma migrate deploy'
Startup reconciliation: any startup_locks with expires_at < now are deleted
Startup reconciliation: any message_queue rows with status=PROCESSING and updated_at < now-2min are reset to PENDING
Startup reconciliation: any tenant rows with status=STARTING or PROVISIONING are set to FAILED and error_message='Process crashed during startup'
Startup reconciliation: a SYSTEM_STARTUP audit log entry is written after reconciliation completes
Fastify listens on CONTROL_PLANE_PORT and logs 'Control plane started' at info level
GET /health returns HTTP 200 with {ok: true, uptime: <ms>}
Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM closes Fastify then Prisma client
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: tenant provisioning endpoint with directory creation
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to call POST /v1/tenants/provision so that a new tenant record is created and their isolated directory structure is set up on disk.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/tenants/provision accepts {slackTeamId, slackUserId} and returns {tenantId, status}
tenant_id is computed as sha256(team_id + ':' + user_id).slice(0, 16)
If tenant already exists (by principal), returns existing tenant without creating a new one (idempotent)
Creates tenant DB row with status=PROVISIONING, relay_token (crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')), container_name='claw-tenant-<id>', data_dir='/data/tenants/<id>'
Creates directories: /data/tenants/<id>/home, /workspace, /config, /logs, /secrets
Generates per-tenant relay token and writes to /data/tenants/<id>/secrets/relay-token
Sets status=NEW after directory creation (provisioning is triggered separately via start)
If provision_attempts >= 3 and status=FAILED, returns HTTP 409 with error 'Max provision attempts reached'
TENANT_PROVISIONED audit event is written on success
TENANT_PROVISION_FAILED audit event and status=FAILED are written on any error; directories are cleaned up (rollback)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: workspace template seeding with AGENTS.md merge logic
userStories.description: As the system, I want workspace templates to be seeded into new tenant workspaces so that tenants start with the required operational files.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/workspace/AGENTS.md exists in the repo with a '## Task Execution' section containing the exact text from §8.2.1
A seedWorkspace(tenantId, workspacePath) function copies all files from templates/workspace/ into the tenant workspace
If AGENTS.md does not exist in tenant workspace: file is copied directly from template
If AGENTS.md exists and already contains '## Task Execution': file is left untouched
If AGENTS.md exists but is missing '## Task Execution': the section is appended verbatim to the end of the file
seedWorkspace is called during provisioning flow (step 5 from §8.2) before docker run
Template source path is resolved relative to /opt/claw-orchestrator/templates/workspace/ (configurable via TEMPLATES_DIR env var)
Unit tests cover all three AGENTS.md merge scenarios
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: startup lock mechanism using SQLite as distributed mutex
userStories.description: As the control plane, I want a startup lock so that at most one container start sequence runs per tenant at a time, preventing race conditions when multiple messages arrive simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: acquireStartupLock(tenantId, requestId) inserts a row into startup_locks with locked_by=requestId and expires_at=now+5min inside a SQLite transaction
If INSERT fails (row exists) and expires_at >= now: returns {acquired: false}
If INSERT fails and expires_at < now (stale): deletes old row and retries INSERT once; returns {acquired: true} on success
releaseStartupLock(tenantId, requestId) deletes the row WHERE tenant_id=? AND locked_by=?
releaseStartupLock is always called in a finally block in the startup sequence
Non-holder behavior: when lock is not acquired, message is enqueued and tenant status is polled every 2 seconds for up to 3 minutes
If status never reaches ACTIVE within 3 minutes, returns {queued: true, message: 'tenant is starting, please wait'}
Unit tests verify: successful lock acquire, blocked duplicate acquire, stale lock takeover
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: health polling loop for tenant containers
userStories.description: As the control plane, I want to poll the tenant health endpoint after container start so that I know when the tenant is ready to accept messages.
acceptanceCriteria: pollUntilHealthy(tenantId, containerName) polls GET http://<containerName>:3101/health every 2 seconds
Each poll request has a 3-second timeout
Polling stops and returns 'healthy' when HTTP 200 response has ok=true
Polling stops and returns 'timeout' after 90 seconds without ok=true
On healthy: tenant status is set to ACTIVE, last_started_at is set to now, TENANT_STARTED audit event is written
On timeout during provisioning: provisioning rollback is triggered (see US-019)
On timeout for a previously-ACTIVE tenant: status is set to UNHEALTHY, TENANT_UNHEALTHY audit event is written
Health polling runs in the background and does not block the HTTP response to the caller
Unit tests mock HTTP calls and verify state transitions for both success and timeout cases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: tenant wake-up flow (POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/start)
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to call POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/start so that a stopped tenant container is restarted and brought back to ACTIVE state.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/start returns HTTP 202 immediately with {status: 'starting'}
Acquires startup lock before calling docker start; if lock not acquired, returns {status: 'already_starting'}
Checks MAX_ACTIVE_TENANTS cap before starting; if at cap, sets queued_for_start_at and returns {status: 'queued'}
Calls dockerStart('claw-tenant-<id>') via docker-client package
Sets tenant status=STARTING and last_started_at=now in DB
Starts pollUntilHealthy in background; updates to ACTIVE on success or UNHEALTHY on timeout
If tenant status is already ACTIVE, returns {status: 'active'} without starting again (idempotent)
If tenant status is DELETING, returns HTTP 409
IMAGE_UPDATED audit event is written if image_tag changed from the previously recorded tag
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: message forwarding endpoint (POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/message)
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to forward a message to an active tenant runtime so that the tenant's OpenClaw agent can process it and return a response.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/message accepts a RelayMessageRequest body and returns RelayMessageResponse
Validates X-Relay-Token header against tenant.relay_token; returns HTTP 401 if mismatch
If tenant status is not ACTIVE, returns HTTP 503 with {ok: false, error: 'Tenant not active'}
Forwards request to POST http://<containerName>:3100/message with X-Relay-Token header
Request to tenant runtime has a 4-minute timeout
On HTTP 200 from runtime: updates tenant.last_activity_at=now, writes MESSAGE_DELIVERED audit event, returns response
On non-200 or timeout: increments message attempts, writes MESSAGE_FAILED if attempts >= 3, returns {ok: false, error: ...}
Container hostname is derived as 'claw-tenant-<tenantId>' without a DB lookup (derived from tenantId)
Pino logs include tenant_id, slack_event_id, and duration_ms for each forwarded message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: tenant stop endpoint (POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/stop)
userStories.description: As an admin or scheduler, I want to call POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/stop so that a tenant container is gracefully stopped and the tenant is marked STOPPED.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /v1/tenants/:tenantId/stop returns HTTP 200 with {status: 'stopped'}
Calls dockerStop('claw-tenant-<id>', 10) with 10-second timeout
Sets tenant status=STOPPED and last_stopped_at=now in DB
Clears queued_for_start_at if set
Writes TENANT_STOPPED audit event with actor='system' or 'admin:<user>' depending on caller
If tenant is already STOPPED, returns {status: 'already_stopped'} without calling docker
If tenant does not exist, returns HTTP 404
If dockerStop fails (container not found), still updates DB to STOPPED and logs a warning
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: tenant deletion endpoint (DELETE /v1/tenants/:tenantId)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to call DELETE /v1/tenants/:tenantId so that a tenant's container and resources are fully cleaned up following the offboarding flow from §12.
acceptanceCriteria: DELETE /v1/tenants/:tenantId immediately sets status=DELETING and deletion_requested_at=now
Stops container if running: dockerStop('claw-tenant-<id>', 10)
Removes container: dockerRm('claw-tenant-<id>')
Archives /data/tenants/<id>/ to /data/tenants-archive/<id>/ (move, not copy)
Purges all message_queue rows for this tenant
Purges startup_locks row for this tenant if present
Soft-deletes tenant row by setting deleted_at=now (row is NOT hard-deleted)
Writes TENANT_DELETED audit event
Returns HTTP 200 with {deleted: true} after all steps complete
If tenant is already DELETING or deleted, returns HTTP 409
If archive directory already exists, appends a timestamp suffix to avoid collision
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: allowlist enforcement with admin CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As the system, I want allowlist gating so that only authorized Slack users can provision tenants, with admin endpoints to grant and revoke access.
acceptanceCriteria: isAllowed(slackTeamId, slackUserId) queries allowlist WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND slack_team_id=? AND (slack_user_id=? OR slack_user_id IS NULL)
Returns true if any matching row exists, false otherwise
POST /v1/admin/allowlist accepts {slack_team_id, slack_user_id?, added_by, note?} and inserts a row; returns {id, created_at}
If slack_user_id is omitted, the entire team is allowed (null slack_user_id in DB)
DELETE /v1/admin/allowlist/:id sets revoked_at=now on the row; returns {revoked: true}
ACCESS_GRANTED audit event written on POST; ACCESS_REVOKED written on DELETE
Relay calls isAllowed before provisioning; if denied: posts rejection DM to Slack and writes ACCESS_DENIED audit event
For existing tenants whose allowlist entry is revoked: message delivery returns {ok: false, error: 'Access revoked'}
Unit tests cover: team-level allow, user-level allow, revoked entry, no entry
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: UNHEALTHY state detection, recovery, and user notification
userStories.description: As the system, I want automatic detection and recovery from UNHEALTHY tenant state so that transient failures are handled transparently without losing queued messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Health polling detects UNHEALTHY when tenant was ACTIVE and health endpoint returns non-200 for 3 consecutive polls (6 seconds)
Sets status=UNHEALTHY, writes TENANT_UNHEALTHY audit event with reason field
Posts to user Slack DM: 'Your workspace is experiencing issues. We're attempting to recover it automatically.'
After 30-second cooldown, auto-recovery attempts the wake-up flow (dockerStart + pollUntilHealthy)
If recovery succeeds within 90 seconds: sets status=ACTIVE, writes TENANT_RECOVERED audit event, replays queued messages
If recovery fails: leaves status=UNHEALTHY, posts to user: 'We were unable to recover your workspace automatically. Please try again in a few minutes.'
Auto-recovery is attempted at most once automatically; subsequent attempts require admin POST /v1/tenants/:id/start
New messages while UNHEALTHY are enqueued (not dropped)
Unit tests verify: UNHEALTHY transition on 3 consecutive poll failures, recovery success path, recovery failure path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: audit log writes for all system events
userStories.description: As an operator, I want an append-only audit log so that I can trace all significant system events for debugging, compliance, and incident response.
acceptanceCriteria: writeAuditLog({tenantId?, eventType, actor, metadata?}) inserts a row into audit_log with a UUID v4 id and created_at=Date.now()
All 18 event types from §25 are used at their correct call sites across control-plane, relay, and scheduler
GET /v1/admin/audit accepts query params: tenant_id, event_type, limit (default 100, max 500), before (Unix ms timestamp)
Returns {events: AuditLog[], total: number} ordered by created_at DESC
Audit log rows are never deleted or updated
metadata is stored as JSON string in the TEXT column and parsed on read
Integration test: provision a tenant, then call GET /v1/admin/audit?tenant_id=<id> and verify TENANT_PROVISIONED event appears
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: provisioning failure rollback
userStories.description: As the system, I want provisioning failures to roll back completely so that a failed tenant always reaches a consistent FAILED state with no orphaned resources.
acceptanceCriteria: A rollbackProvisioning(tenantId, error) function exists that: sets status=FAILED and error_message=error.message, increments provision_attempts
rollbackProvisioning removes /data/tenants/<id>/ directory tree with rm -rf if it exists
rollbackProvisioning calls dockerRm('claw-tenant-<id>') if the container exists (ignores 'not found' errors)
rollbackProvisioning writes a TENANT_PROVISION_FAILED audit event with error details
rollbackProvisioning is called in a catch block wrapping the entire provisioning sequence
If provision_attempts < 3 after rollback, a subsequent provisioning call retries fresh
If provision_attempts >= 3, status remains FAILED permanently and the relay posts the failure DM to user
Failure DM text: 'Sorry, I wasn't able to set up your workspace. Our team has been notified. Please try again in a few minutes.'
Unit tests verify rollback is called on each failure step (directory creation fails, docker run fails, health timeout)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: container image tracking and admin promote endpoint
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to track container image versions and promote new images so that tenant containers are updated lazily on next restart.
acceptanceCriteria: container_images table is seeded with an initial row for the default image tag from TENANT_IMAGE env var with is_default=1
POST /v1/admin/images/:id/promote sets the target row is_default=1 and all other rows is_default=0 in a single transaction
GET /v1/admin/images returns all rows from container_images ordered by created_at DESC
getDefaultImage() queries SELECT * FROM container_images WHERE is_default=1 LIMIT 1 and is used by provisioning and start flows
When a tenant container is started, tenants.image_tag is set to the current default image tag
If image_tag changed from previous recorded value, an IMAGE_UPDATED audit event is written
Deprecated images have deprecated_at set when a newer image is promoted
A canary validation step is documented in code comments (actual canary execution is manual for MVP)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Control plane: resource limits applied per container via docker run flags
userStories.description: As the system, I want Docker resource limits applied to every tenant container so that no single tenant can exhaust host CPU, memory, or process resources.
acceptanceCriteria: dockerRun for tenant containers includes: --cpus=1.0, --memory=1536m, --memory-swap=1536m, --pids-limit=256, --ulimit nofile=1024:1024
resource_overrides JSON on the tenant row is parsed and merged over defaults before calling dockerRun
resource_overrides supports fields: cpus (number), memory_mb (number), pids_limit (number)
auth-profiles.json is bind-mounted read-only: -v ~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json:/root/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json:ro
Tenant data dirs are bind-mounted: home dir → /home/agent, workspace → /workspace, config → /home/agent/.config
Environment variables set in container: HOME=/home/agent, XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/home/agent/.config, XDG_CACHE_HOME=/home/agent/.cache, XDG_STATE_HOME=/home/agent/.local/state
Container name is set to 'claw-tenant-<tenantId>'
Unit test verifies that dockerRun is called with correct flags for both default and override cases
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack relay: Fastify app scaffold with Pino logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working Fastify app for the Slack relay so that it starts cleanly, connects to the control plane, and is ready to receive Slack events.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/slack-relay/src/index.ts creates a Fastify app with Pino logger in JSON format
App loads slack-relay config via shared-config and fails fast on missing env vars
GET /health returns HTTP 200 with {ok: true}
On startup, relay performs a startup sweep: any message_queue rows with status=PROCESSING older than 2 minutes are reset to PENDING via control plane API
App listens on SLACK_RELAY_PORT and logs 'Slack relay started' at info level
All outbound HTTP calls to control plane use CONTROL_PLANE_URL base URL
Pino log lines include service='slack-relay', tenant_id (when known), slack_event_id, request_id
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM closes Fastify
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack relay: Slack event signature verification
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to verify Slack event signatures so that only genuine Slack webhook payloads are processed.
acceptanceCriteria: verifySlackSignature(rawBody, headers) computes HMAC-SHA256 of 'v0:<timestamp>:<rawBody>' using SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET
Compares computed signature with X-Slack-Signature header using timingSafeEqual
Rejects requests where X-Slack-Request-Timestamp is more than 5 minutes old (replay attack prevention)
Returns HTTP 403 with {error: 'Invalid signature'} if verification fails
Raw body is captured before Fastify parses JSON (using addContentTypeParser or preParsing hook)
Verification is applied as a Fastify preHandler hook on POST /slack/events
Unit tests cover: valid signature, invalid signature, expired timestamp, missing headers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack relay: tenant routing with immediate 200 ack and async processing
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to immediately acknowledge Slack events with HTTP 200 and process them asynchronously so that Slack's 3-second timeout is never exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /slack/events returns HTTP 200 with {} body immediately before any async processing begins
Handles Slack URL verification challenge: if body has type='url_verification', returns {challenge: body.challenge} synchronously
Extracts team_id and user_id from Slack event envelope
Computes tenant_id as sha256(team_id + ':' + user_id).slice(0, 16) using Node crypto module
Checks allowlist via control plane API; if denied, posts rejection DM via Slack chat.postMessage and writes ACCESS_DENIED log
Rejection DM text: 'Thanks for your interest! This system is currently invite-only. Contact [admin contact] to request access.'
If tenant does not exist: calls POST /v1/tenants/provision to create record
All subsequent processing (start, queue, forward) runs in a detached async function after the 200 is sent
Deduplication: slack_event_id is inserted as UNIQUE in message_queue; duplicate Slack retries are silently ignored
Unit tests verify 200 is returned before provision/start calls complete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack relay: message queuing and ordered replay when tenant is stopped or starting
userStories.description: As the Slack relay, I want to queue messages when the tenant is not active and replay them in order once the tenant becomes healthy so that no messages are dropped during startup.
acceptanceCriteria: On receiving a Slack event, relay inserts a message_queue row with status=PENDING and the full raw Slack event JSON as payload
If tenant status is STOPPED: relay calls POST /v1/tenants/:id/start to wake tenant, then waits for ACTIVE status
If tenant status is STARTING: relay does not call start again, polls status every 2 seconds for up to 3 minutes
Once tenant is ACTIVE: relay fetches all PENDING messages for tenant ordered by created_at ASC and delivers them sequentially
Before forwarding each message, sets status=PROCESSING; on success sets DELIVERED; on failure after 3 attempts sets FAILED
Only one message per tenant is in PROCESSING state at a time (sequential delivery per tenant)
If status never reaches ACTIVE after 3 minutes, posts 'tenant is starting, please wait' to Slack
On relay process restart, any PROCESSING rows older than 2 minutes are reset to PENDING
Integration test: queue 3 messages while tenant is STOPPED, verify all 3 are delivered in order after wake
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack relay: '⏳ Working on it' interim message at 15s timeout and 4-minute max wait
userStories.description: As a Slack user, I want to receive an interim message if my request takes longer than 15 seconds so that I know the system is working and haven't been ignored.
acceptanceCriteria: When forwarding a message to the tenant runtime, a 15-second timer is started
If no response is received within 15 seconds, relay calls Slack chat.postMessage with text '⏳ Working on it...' in the user's DM channel
The interim message is sent only once per message delivery attempt
Relay continues waiting for the tenant response for up to 4 minutes total (240 seconds)
On receiving the final response, relay calls chat.postMessage with the actual response text (or blocks if blocks field is non-null)
If the 4-minute timeout is reached with no response, relay posts 'I'm still working on this. I'll follow up when complete.' and marks the queue item as FAILED
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN from config is used for all chat.postMessage calls
Channel is the user's DM channel (resolved from the original Slack event)
Unit tests mock chat.postMessage and verify interim message is sent on 15s timer fire
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scheduler: service scaffold with idle tenant stop (48-hour inactivity)
userStories.description: As the system, I want a scheduler service that runs periodic jobs so that idle tenants are automatically stopped after 48 hours of inactivity.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/scheduler/src/index.ts starts a recurring job loop using setInterval with SCHEDULER_INTERVAL_MS (default 60 seconds)
Idle stop job queries: SELECT * FROM tenants WHERE status='ACTIVE' AND deleted_at IS NULL AND last_activity_at < (now - IDLE_STOP_HOURS * 3600000)
For each idle tenant, calls POST /v1/tenants/:id/stop via control plane API
Logs each idle stop with tenant_id and idle_duration_ms at info level
TENANT_STOPPED audit event is written with actor='scheduler' by the control plane
Scheduler does not stop a tenant that is in STARTING or PROVISIONING state
Scheduler logs 'Scheduler started, interval=<ms>ms' on startup
Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM stops the interval and exits cleanly
Unit test verifies idle stop query selects correct tenants based on last_activity_at threshold
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scheduler: per-tenant disk quota enforcement via du sampling every 5 minutes
userStories.description: As the system, I want disk usage to be monitored per tenant every 5 minutes so that tenants approaching or exceeding quota receive warnings and message delivery is blocked when quota is exceeded.
acceptanceCriteria: Disk check job runs every 5 minutes (separate interval from idle stop, or same loop with counter)
For each active tenant, runs 'du -sb /data/tenants/<id>/' via execa and parses byte count
Quota defaults: 10 GB workspace + 2 GB home = 12 GB total per tenant
At 90% of quota (10.8 GB): writes DISK_QUOTA_WARNING audit event, posts Slack message: 'You can free space by clearing build caches: rm -rf ~/.cache/ and /workspace/node_modules/'
At 100% of quota (12 GB): writes DISK_QUOTA_EXCEEDED audit event, sets disk_quota_exceeded=1 on tenant row, posts Slack error message
When disk_quota_exceeded=1: control plane blocks new message delivery with {ok: false, error: 'Disk quota exceeded'}
When usage drops below 95% of quota: clears disk_quota_exceeded=0 automatically on next check
Host-level check: if /data mount is >80% full, logs warning; if >95% full, sets all ACTIVE tenants to UNHEALTHY
Unit tests mock du output and verify correct threshold actions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scheduler: message queue reaping and stale startup lock sweep
userStories.description: As the system, I want old queue rows and stale locks cleaned up periodically so that the database does not grow unbounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue reaping job runs hourly (or on every scheduler tick for simplicity): deletes DELIVERED rows older than 7 days
Queue reaping job deletes FAILED rows older than 30 days
Stale lock sweep: deletes startup_locks WHERE expires_at < now (runs on every tick)
Archive cleanup job: removes /data/tenants-archive/<id>/ directories older than 30 days
Audit log archive job: when audit_log table exceeds 100,000 rows, exports rows older than 90 days to /data/audit-archive/audit-YYYY-MM.ndjson.gz and deletes them from DB
All reaping operations log count of rows affected at debug level
Reaping runs atomically in SQLite transactions to avoid partial deletes
Unit tests verify: 7-day DELIVERED reaping, 30-day FAILED reaping, stale lock removal
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Scheduler: capacity queue retry when ACTIVE_TENANTS_OVERFLOW_POLICY=queue
userStories.description: As a queued Slack user, I want my tenant to automatically start when capacity becomes available so that I don't have to resend my message.
acceptanceCriteria: Every 60 seconds, scheduler checks for tenants WHERE status='STOPPED' AND queued_for_start_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL
For each queued tenant, checks current active count via getActiveCount()
If active count < MAX_ACTIVE_TENANTS: calls POST /v1/tenants/:id/start for the tenant with earliest queued_for_start_at
On successful start: clears queued_for_start_at, processes any pending messages in message_queue
Only starts one queued tenant per scheduler tick to avoid overshoot of capacity cap
ACTIVE_TENANTS_OVERFLOW_POLICY=reject disables this retry logic
Unit tests verify: queued tenant starts when capacity opens, respects one-at-a-time limit, respects policy=reject
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tenant Docker image: Dockerfile with OpenClaw, required CLIs, and non-root agent user
userStories.description: As the control plane, I want a buildable tenant container image so that each tenant runs OpenClaw with all required tools in an isolated environment.
acceptanceCriteria: docker/tenant-image/Dockerfile starts from a Node.js 22 LTS base image (Debian-based for apt access)
Installs: git, openssh-client, curl, jq, gh (GitHub CLI), and shell tools
Installs OpenClaw via npm install -g openclaw (or equivalent)
Installs Claude Code (claude) via npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Installs OpenAI Codex via npm install -g @openai/codex
Installs vercel CLI via npm install -g vercel
Installs convex CLI via npm install -g convex
Creates a non-root 'agent' user with home at /home/agent
Sets WORKDIR to /home/agent and default USER to agent
Dockerfile does NOT set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or embed any auth credentials
Image is tagged as claw-tenant:sha-<7char> via build arg IMAGE_TAG
Multi-arch build is supported: buildx with --platform linux/arm64,linux/amd64
docker build succeeds without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tenant image: entrypoint script with auth-profiles.json verification and container startup
userStories.description: As the system, I want the tenant container to start with a verified entrypoint that checks for the required auth-profiles.json bind-mount and starts all required processes.
acceptanceCriteria: docker/tenant-image/entrypoint.sh is a bash script set as the container ENTRYPOINT

Entrypoint checks if /root/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json exists; if not, logs ERROR 'auth-profiles.json is missing. Model calls will fail. Ensure the host bind-mount is configured.' and continues (does not exit)
Entrypoint creates required directories: /home/agent/.ssh (mode 700), /home/agent/.config, /home/agent/.cache, /home/agent/.local/state, /workspace
Validates .ssh permissions: chmod 700 /home/agent/.ssh, chmod 600 any private key files
Sets environment: HOME=/home/agent, XDG_CONFIG_HOME=/home/agent/.config, XDG_CACHE_HOME=/home/agent/.cache, XDG_STATE_HOME=/home/agent/.local/state
Starts the health server on port 3101 in the background (see US-033)
Starts the message server on port 3100 in the background (see US-034)
Starts OpenClaw as the main process (exec openclaw or equivalent)
Entrypoint is tested by running 'docker run --rm <image> bash -c "exit 0"' successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tenant container: health endpoint server on port 3101
userStories.description: As the control plane, I want the tenant container to expose a health endpoint on port 3101 so that I can determine when the tenant is ready to accept messages.
acceptanceCriteria: A small Node.js HTTP server (or bash+socat script) runs inside the container on port 3101
GET /health checks: (1) openclaw process is running via pgrep or PID file, (2) /workspace is writable (fs.accessSync with W_OK), (3) /home/agent is writable
If all three checks pass: returns HTTP 200 with {ok: true, status: 'healthy', checks: {openclaw: true, workspace_mounted: true, home_mounted: true}, uptime_ms: <number>}
If any check fails: returns HTTP 503 with {ok: false, status: 'starting', checks: {openclaw: <bool>, workspace_mounted: <bool>, home_mounted: <bool>}}
Health server source lives in docker/tenant-image/health-server.js or health-server.sh
Health server starts before OpenClaw in the entrypoint script
Content-Type header is application/json on all responses
curl http://localhost:3101/health inside a running container returns 200 within 5 seconds of start
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tenant container: message endpoint server on port 3100
userStories.description: As the Slack relay (via control plane), I want the tenant container to expose a message endpoint on port 3100 so that Slack messages can be forwarded to the OpenClaw agent.
acceptanceCriteria: A Node.js HTTP server runs inside the container on port 3100
POST /message validates X-Relay-Token header against the RELAY_TOKEN environment variable; returns HTTP 401 if mismatch
RELAY_TOKEN is injected as an environment variable by the control plane when running the container
Accepted request body matches RelayMessageRequest schema from shared-types
Server forwards the message to OpenClaw (via stdin pipe, IPC, or openclaw CLI invocation) and awaits a response
Returns HTTP 200 with {ok: true, response: '<agent reply>', blocks: null} on success
Returns HTTP 500 with {ok: false, error: '<message>'} if OpenClaw returns an error or times out internally
Message server source lives in docker/tenant-image/message-server.js
Logs each received message with messageId and slackEventId at info level
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workspace template files with required AGENTS.md Task Execution section
userStories.description: As the system, I want a workspace template directory committed to the repo so that all new tenants start with the correct operational guidance pre-seeded in their workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/workspace/ directory exists in the repo root

templates/workspace/AGENTS.md exists and contains a '## Task Execution' section with exact text from §8.2.1: 'For any task that is complex enough to take more than ~2 minutes:
- Spawn a sub-agent or background process to handle it
- Don't block the conversation
- When it's done, report back with a concise summary of what was done'
templates/workspace/AGENTS.md is a well-formed markdown file (parseable without errors)
Template directory is referenced in the deployment layout at /opt/claw-orchestrator/templates/workspace/
TEMPLATES_DIR environment variable defaults to <repo-root>/templates in development and /opt/claw-orchestrator/templates in production
README or inline comment explains the merge behavior on re-provisioning (copy vs append)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: systemd unit files for all three services with correct dependency ordering
userStories.description: As an operator, I want systemd unit files for all three services so that they start automatically on boot in the correct order and restart on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/systemd/claw-control-plane.service exists with: After=network.target docker.service, Requires=docker.service, User=claw, WorkingDirectory=/opt/claw-orchestrator/apps/control-plane, ExecStart=/usr/bin/node dist/index.js, Restart=always, RestartSec=5, EnvironmentFile=/opt/claw-orchestrator/.env, StandardOutput=journal, SyslogIdentifier=claw-control-plane
deploy/systemd/claw-slack-relay.service exists with same structure, After=claw-control-plane.service
deploy/systemd/claw-scheduler.service exists with same structure, independent of relay
All unit files have [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target
A deploy/scripts/install-services.sh script copies unit files to /etc/systemd/system/, runs systemctl daemon-reload, and enables all three services
A deploy/scripts/update.sh script implements the update procedure from §31: git pull, pnpm install, pnpm build, prisma migrate deploy, systemctl restart for all three
Unit file comments document the startup order from §31
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Host-side directory layout setup script and tenant-shell interactive access helper
userStories.description: As an operator, I want setup scripts that create the required directory structure and a tenant-shell helper for interactive access so that the system can be deployed and debugged easily.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy/scripts/setup-dirs.sh creates all required directories with correct ownership: /data/claw-orchestrator, /data/tenants, /data/tenants-archive, /data/backups, /data/audit-archive, /opt/claw-orchestrator (owned by claw user)
deploy/scripts/setup-dirs.sh creates the 'claw' system user if it does not exist
scripts/tenant-shell.sh accepts a tenant_id argument and runs: docker exec -it --user agent claw-tenant-<tenant_id> /bin/bash
scripts/tenant-shell.sh validates that the container is running before exec and prints a helpful error if not
deploy/scripts/backup.sh implements the backup procedure from §30: SQLite checkpoint, copy db.sqlite to /data/backups/<date>/, tar.gz tenant data excluding caches, optional S3 sync
A systemd timer file deploy/systemd/claw-backup.timer is provided for daily 03:00 UTC backups
All scripts are executable (chmod +x) and have a #!/bin/bash shebang
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: test-utils package and unit tests for core logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a test-utils package with test fixtures and helpers so that unit tests can be written quickly with consistent mocks and assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/test-utils/src/fixtures.ts exports makeTenant(overrides?) returning a valid Tenant object, makeMessageQueueRow(overrides?) returning a valid MessageQueueRow
packages/test-utils/src/mocks.ts exports mockPrismaClient() returning a vitest mock of the Prisma Client with all used methods mocked
packages/test-utils/src/mocks.ts exports mockDockerClient() returning a mock of the docker-client package
Unit tests in apps/control-plane/tests/unit/ cover: tenant state transitions (all valid and invalid transitions), startup lock acquire/release/expire, capacity cap enforcement, allowlist query logic
Unit tests in apps/slack-relay/tests/unit/ cover: Slack signature verification (valid, invalid, expired timestamp), tenant ID computation (sha256(team:user).slice(0,16))
Unit tests in apps/scheduler/tests/unit/ cover: idle stop eligibility (last_activity_at threshold), disk quota threshold actions
All unit tests run with 'pnpm test' and pass
Test coverage for core state machine functions is >= 80%
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration tests for control plane API with real SQLite and mocked Docker
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests that exercise the full control plane API with a real in-memory SQLite database so that API contracts and DB interactions are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/ contains a test suite that starts a real Fastify control plane instance with a real in-memory SQLite DB
Docker calls are mocked at the docker-client package boundary (no real containers started)
Test: POST /v1/tenants/provision creates tenant row with status=NEW and correct tenant_id
Test: POST /v1/tenants/provision is idempotent (calling twice with same team+user returns same tenant_id)
Test: POST /v1/tenants/:id/start transitions status from STOPPED to STARTING, then to ACTIVE after mock health poll returns ok
Test: POST /v1/tenants/:id/stop transitions status to STOPPED
Test: DELETE /v1/tenants/:id sets deleted_at and writes TENANT_DELETED audit log entry
Test: allowlist check blocks provisioning when no matching allowlist row exists
Test: MAX_ACTIVE_TENANTS=1 blocks second tenant start with status=queued
All integration tests run with 'pnpm test:integration' and pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: End-to-end integration test: first message provisions tenant and returns response
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a full end-to-end test that simulates a first Slack message from a new user so that the complete provisioning and message delivery flow is verified automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/first-message.test.ts sends a fake Slack event HTTP POST to the relay's /slack/events endpoint with valid signature
Test pre-seeds an allowlist entry for the test user's team_id and user_id
Test verifies: relay returns HTTP 200 immediately (before provisioning completes)
Test verifies: control plane tenant row transitions through PROVISIONING → STARTING → ACTIVE states
Test verifies: tenant directories were created at DATA_DIR/<tenantId>/home, /workspace, /config, /logs, /secrets
Test verifies: AGENTS.md was seeded in the tenant workspace containing '## Task Execution'
Test verifies: message_queue row was created with status=DELIVERED after processing
Test verifies: Slack chat.postMessage was called with the tenant's response (mocked Slack API)
Test verifies: audit log contains TENANT_PROVISIONED, TENANT_STARTED, MESSAGE_DELIVERED events in order
Test verifies: sending a second message from the same user reuses the same tenant_id (no new provisioning)
All E2E tests run with 'pnpm test:e2e' and pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bind-mount host Claude Code credentials into tenant containers
userStories.description: As the system, I want each tenant container to have access to the host Claude Code OAuth credentials so that the claude CLI works inside tenant containers without per-tenant auth setup.
acceptanceCriteria: docker run for provisioning includes -v ~/.claude/.credentials.json:/root/.claude/.credentials.json:ro bind mount alongside auth-profiles.json
Entrypoint script checks both /root/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json AND /root/.claude/.credentials.json exist and are non-empty; exits with clear error if either is missing
Unit test: provisioning service includes .credentials.json in docker run bind mounts list
Unit test: entrypoint validation exits non-zero with descriptive error when .credentials.json is absent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bake Ralph skills into tenant Docker image at build time
userStories.description: As a tenant, I want the 6 Ralph agent skills pre-installed in my container so I can use them without any runtime setup or network dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: docker/tenant-image/skills/ contains all 6 skills: prd, ralph, ralph-codex-loop, qa-plan-generator, qa-plan-json, qa-codex-loop
Dockerfile includes COPY skills/ /root/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/ step
scripts/update-ralph-skills.sh exists, is executable, and syncs skills from a given ralph repo path
After docker build, running 'openclaw skills list' inside the container shows all 6 ralph skills as ready
Typecheck passes
